reasoning: Nvidia suffered a significant sell-off in early March 2025, dropping roughly $1 trillion in market cap to around $2.85 trillion (stock price ~$116). Meanwhile, Apple appears to remain comfortably above $3.3 trillion. For Nvidia to overtake Apple by May 30, it would need to close a ~$500B+ gap, requiring at least a 15-25% relative outperformance in just two months. While strong momentum currently favors Apple and works against Nvidia, a major wildcard is Nvidia's Q1 earnings report, which historically occurs in the final week of May (e.g., May 22). This perfectly aligns with the May 31 resolution date. If Nvidia delivers a blowout quarter and strong forward guidance, a massive 15%+ post-earnings jump is possible, propelling it back to the #1 spot. Taking all this into account, I find the 10% crowd forecast to be well-calibrated, but I am nudging it up slightly to 12% to account for the high volatility of the late-May earnings catalyst.
